children.text: Sections 1106 and 1107(a) of this title (relating to prohibited transactions) shall not apply—

children.tail: , to a loan of money or other extension of credit between a plan and a party in interest under a binding contract in effect on

(or pursuant to renewals of such a contract), if such loan or other extension of credit remains at least as favorable to the plan as an arm’s-length transaction with an unrelated party would be, and if the execution of the contract, the making of the loan, or the extension of credit was not, at the time of such execution, making, or extension, a prohibited transaction (within the meaning of
or the corresponding provisions of prior law);
, to a lease or joint use of property involving the plan and a party in interest pursuant to a binding contract in effect on

(or pursuant to renewals of such a contract), if such lease or joint use remains at least as favorable to the plan as an arm’s-length transaction with an unrelated party would be and if the execution of the contract was not, at the time of such execution, a prohibited transaction (within the meaning of
or the corresponding provisions of prior law);
, to the sale, exchange or other disposition of property described in paragraph (2) between a plan and a party in interest if—

children.text: in the case of a sale, exchange, or other disposition of the property by the plan to the party in interest, the plan receives an amount which is not less than the fair market value of the property at the time of such disposition; and
in the case of the acquisition of the property by the plan, the plan pays an amount which is not in excess of the fair market value of the property at the time of such acquisition;
